assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and internet access.
No platform account, credentials, or product resources exist yet.

developer_goal: Create a Checkly account and run a hosted API check against an HTTP endpoint.

first_success.outcome: A saved API check sends its request and passes its assertions.
first_success.observable_signal: The Checkly dashboard displays a successful check result for the configured URL.

route_selection.rule: Prefer a hosted or direct API route over local installation, and prefer documented cURL when it reaches the same first success.

candidates.name: Dashboard quickstart: first API check
candidates.reason: The dashboard API-check quickstart is hosted and avoids the Monitoring as Code CLI setup.

candidates.name: Monitoring as Code CLI
candidates.reason: It adds local Node.js and CLI configuration that the hosted dashboard route does not require.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to Checkly and open the dashboard.
Create an API check and enter its name, request method, and URL.
Save the API check with the quickstart assertion settings.
required_path.observable_result: The check exists in the dashboard.

required_path.action: Run the check from the dashboard.

required_path.observable_result: A passing check result is displayed.

prerequisites.description: An HTTP API endpoint that Checkly can reach.

external_gates.description: The target API must be reachable from Checkly's hosted locations.

platform_outcomes.description: A saved API check sends its request and passes its assertions.

excluded.reason: These occur after the first observable end-to-end result.

uncertainties.question: Authenticated UI labels can change independently of the documented quickstart.
uncertainties.impact: The required path may differ for some fresh accounts.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account walkthrough through the authenticated first-success state.
